Tilera Multicore Components (TMC™) Library

Detailed Description

Utility routines for parallel program development.

The Tilera Multicore Components API provides many useful primitives for building parallel programs. These APIs include routines for managing the binding of tasks to cores, allocating memory with particular performance characteristics, communicating between tasks, and much more. All of the API components are designed for easy integration into pre-existing applications.

TMC has the following attributes:

  • Process model agnostic: The TMC API components can be integrated into whatever task model your application requires. TMC does not require you to use any particular API to create new tasks, so it is easily integrated into applications that already have their own process or thread management code. TMC works with both thread-based and process-based applications.

  • Componentized: Each API component can be used independently from the others. For example, using the User Dynamic Network (UDN) does not require the use of the iLib process model.

  • Accessible Source Code: Tilera provides source code for all of the TMC components. Since the API is componentized, developers can easily understand the internals of any particular component without having to understand the rest of TMC.

TMC provides the following components:


Modules

 Specified-Attribute Memory Page Allocation (tmc/alloc.h)
 Routines for allocating pages with specified home cache, memory controller, and length.
 "Common Memory" APIs for tmc_shmem (tmc/cmem.h)
 Additional cmem ("common memory") APIs for tmc_shmem.
 CPU Sets and Affinitization (tmc/cpus.h)
 Routines that manage sets of CPUs and control Linux processor affinity.
 User-space Interrupt Installation Routines (tmc/interrupt.h)
 Routines for installing a user-space interrupt with a specific interrupt number and its user-space C callback handler.
 Interprocessor Interrupt Event-handling Routines (tmc/ipi.h)
 Routines for handling IPI events.
 Using mspaces for standard malloc/free (tmc/malloc.h)
 Support for mixing and matching TMC "mspace" library allocation routines with the standard malloc and free API routines.
 Cache Control (tmc/mem.h)
 Support for moving blocks of memory in and out of a core's cache.
 Multiple Heap Allocation (tmc/mspace.h)
 A heap-based memory allocation API that can be used in addition to libc's standard malloc()/free() API.
 Performance Tuning (tmc/perf.h)
 Access performance counters from user space.
 Shared Memory FIFOs (tmc/queue.h)
 Macro-based support for shared memory FIFOs.
 Multi-Process Shared Memory Arenas (tmc/shmem.h)
 Inter-process dynamic shared memory support.
 Spinning Shared Memory Synchronization (tmc/spin.h)
 Spin-based memory synchronization primitives for use with one-task-per-core applications.
 Scheduler Shared Memory Synchronization (tmc/sync.h)
 Routines for performing cross-process shared memory synchronization without requiring pthreads.
 Task Management and Cleanup (tmc/task.h)
 Task management and cleanup operations.
 UDN Helper Routines (tmc/udn.h)
 Simple packets can be sent between tiles over the UDN.

Third-party software: The Tilera IDE makes use of the BeanShell scripting library. Source code for the BeanShell library can be found at the BeanShell website (http://www.beanshell.org/developer.html).

The following is a trademark of Marvell Semiconductor, Inc.: Distributed Switching Architecture (DSA).
